Y. 704 (), AT 12 Tawa, 2007 w205186 (37), WO 18 Wi, 2008 wr.aw.Fr.os0 (3), ardra 11 Fde, 2008, WL 344 (), 78w 7 W€, 2008 WP (), M FL 414 &) TRIF 30 7, 2008, W1, 481 (1) e 26 37, 2008 R L. 579030, RIS 6 3, 2008 TRT foow 7w | MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT AND FORESTS * NOTIFICATION New Delni, the 18th August, 2008 GSR600E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by sections 6 and 25 of the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986 (29 of 1986), the Central Government hereby makes -- 3 of 6 -- THE GAZEFTE OF INDIA ' EXTRAORDINARY [Pagy [I—=Skc. 3(D) the following rules further to amend the Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986, namely:- 1. (1) These rules may be called the Environment (Protection) Seventh Amendment Rules, 2008. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette, 2. In the Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986, In Schedule I, after serial number 100 and entries relating thereto, the following serial number and entries shall be inserted, namely :- S No | Industry | _ Parameter Standard . 2 3 N 4 101 ;;\flnerator A Emission ?fi:‘:fl: B Limiting Sampling Duration concentration | in {minutes) unless in mg/Nm3, stated unless stated Particulate Matter 50 . 30 HCL 50 30 SO, 200 30 ___Co 100 daity average Total Organic Carbon 20 30 Total Dioxins Existing 0.2 8 hours and Incinerator { _ngTEQ/Nm’ Furans * New 0.1 8 hours Inginerator | ngVEQ/Nm® Sb +As + Pb + Cr + Co +Cu 15 2 hours +Mn + Ni + V + their compounds . i *+ The existing piant shali comply with norms for dioxins and furans as 0.1 ng/TEQ/Nm" within a pericd of five years from the date of publication of this netification. -- 4 of 6 -- [ —a@eE3()] TG 51 I ; SR 1 3 4 Notes : vi. vil. All monitored values shall be corrected to 11% oxygen on dry basls. i. The CO; concentration in tail gas shall not be less than 7%. In case, halogenated organic waste is less than 13 by weight in input waste, all the fadlities in single chamber Incinerators shall be designed so as to achieve a minimum temperature of 1100°C, in the indnerator. For fluidized bed technology mcmerator temperature shall be maintained at 950°C. In case halogenated organic waste is more than 1% by welghtin input waste, waste shall be incnerated only in twin chamber incinerators and atl the facliities shall -be designed to achieve 2 minimum temperature of 1100°C in secondary combustion chamber with a gas residence time in secondary combustion chamber not less than two seconds. Scrubber meant for scrubbing emissions shall not be used as quencher. Incineration plants shall be operated (combustion chambers) with such temperature, retention time and turbulence, as to achieve Total Organic Carbon (TCC) content in the slag and bottom ashes jess flh1tan 3%, and their loss on ignitlen is less than 5% of the dry weight. The incinerators shall have a chimney of atleast thirty metre height. B. Wastewater Wastewater (scrubber water and floor washings) shall be discharged into recelving water conforming to the norms prescrived under Schedule VI: General Standards for Discharge of Environment Pollutions (Part A: Effiuents) notified under the Environment (Protection) Rutes, 1986, ii. The built up in Total Dissolved Soilds (TDS) in wastewater of floor washings shall not exceed 1000 mg/l over and above the TDS of raw water used. NS GIAE 2 [F. No. Q-15017/2522007-CPW] RK. VAISH, X.
